   322â†’
   323â†’**Rules:**
   324â†’- Components in `src/components/` â€” flat, no subdirectories (only 4 components)
   325â†’- Business logic in `src/lib/services/` â€” never in components
   326â†’- Stores in `src/lib/stores/` â€” one file per store
   327â†’- Utility functions in `src/lib/utils/` â€” pure functions only, no side effects
   328â†’- Tests co-located: `src/lib/services/connection-manager.test.ts` next to source file
   329â†’
   330â†’### Store Patterns
   331â†’
   332â†’**Nano Store conventions:**
   333â†’
   334â†’```typescript
   335â†’// Store definition pattern (every store file follows this)
   336â†’import { atom } from 'nanostores';
   337â†’import type { PriceTick } from '../types';
   338â†’
   339â†’export const priceStore = atom<PriceTick | null>(null);
   340â†’```
   341â†’
   342â†’**Rules:**
   343â†’- All stores initialize to `null` (not undefined, not default objects)
   344â†’- Only services write to stores â€” never components
   345â†’- Components subscribe in `<script>` blocks using `store.subscribe()`
   346â†’- One store per concern â€” never combine unrelated data in a single store
   347â†’- Type every store with explicit generic: `atom<PriceTick | null>`
   348â†’
   349â†’### Format Patterns
   350â†’
   351â†’**Number Formatting (all handled by `format-utils.ts`):**
   352â†’- Price: `$68,432.17` â€” USD, comma separators, always 2 decimal places
   353â†’- Percentage: `+2.3%` or `-1.8%` â€” sign always shown, 1 decimal place
   354â†’- Absolute change: `+$1,534.22` or `-$892.40` â€” sign + USD, 2 decimal places
   355â†’- Market cap: `$1.34T` or `$892.45B` â€” abbreviated with 2 decimal places
   356â†’- 24h range: `$66.9k â€” $68.9k` â€” abbreviated with 1 decimal place
   357â†’- Chart tooltip: `$68,432.17 at 14:30` â€” full price + 24h time (no date, no seconds)
   358â†’
   359â†’**Rules:**
   360â†’- All formatting goes through `format-utils.ts` helper functions â€” never inline formatting in components
   361â†’- Use `Intl.NumberFormat` as the base â€” no manual string building
   362â†’- Direction indicators always pair symbol with color: `â†‘` green, `â†“` red, `â†’` white (neutral)
   363â†’- Never use color alone to communicate direction
   364â†’
   365â†’### Communication Patterns
   366â†’
   367â†’**Store update flow (unidirectional):**
   368â†’```
   369â†’External API â†’ Service â†’ Store â†’ Component â†’ DOM
   370â†’```
   371â†’
   372â†’**Rules:**
   373â†’- Services are the only writers to stores
   374â†’- Components are read-only subscribers â€” never call `store.set()` from a component
   375â†’- No component-to-component communication â€” always go through stores
   376â†’- No direct DOM manipulation outside of store subscription callbacks in island `<script>` blocks
   377â†’
   378â†’**WebSocket message handling:**
   379â†’- Raw messages are parsed and normalized in `connection-manager.ts` â€” components never see raw WebSocket data
   380â†’- Normalization happens before store write â€” stores only contain clean `PriceTick` objects
   381â†’- Binance format: `{ p: "68432.17", ... }` â†’ `{ price: 68432.17, timestamp: Date.now(), direction: 'up' }`
   382â†’- CoinCap format: `{ bitcoin: "68432.17" }` â†’ same normalized `PriceTick`
   383â†’
   384â†’### Process Patterns
   385â†’
   386â†’**Error Handling:**
   387â†’- WebSocket errors: catch silently, update `statusStore` to `'reconnecting'`, trigger backoff â€” no console.error for expected disconnects
   388â†’- REST errors: catch silently, retain last store values, `console.warn()` with endpoint and status code
   389â†’- Parsing errors (malformed API response): `console.error()` with raw payload, skip the update, do not crash the island
   390â†’- Never throw from a store subscription callback â€” wrap in try/catch
   391â†’- Never show error UI to the user â€” the UX spec explicitly avoids error states. Use stale indicators instead.
   392â†’
   393â†’**Initialization sequence:**
   394â†’- Services initialize in `Layout.astro` via a single `<script>` block that imports and starts `ConnectionManager` and `MarketDataService`
   395â†’- Islands hydrate via `client:load` and immediately subscribe to relevant stores
   396â†’- Order doesn't matter â€” stores start as `null`, components render empty state, data appears when stores update
   397â†’
   398â†’**Animation implementation:**
   399â†’- All durations as constants in a shared location (CSS custom properties or `theme-constants.ts`)
   400â†’- Price flash: CSS transition on `color` property, triggered by toggling a data attribute (`data-flash="up"`)
   401â†’- Entry fade-in: CSS transition on `opacity`, triggered by removing a `data-loading` attribute
   402â†’- Chart draw: lightweight-charts built-in animation API
   403â†’- Always check `prefers-reduced-motion` â€” if enabled, set all durations to 0ms
   404â†’
   405â†’### Enforcement Guidelines
   406â†’
   407â†’**All AI Agents MUST:**
   408â†’1. Follow the file naming and project structure patterns exactly â€” no alternative organizations
   409â†’2. Use `format-utils.ts` for all number display â€” no inline `toFixed()` or manual formatting
   410â†’3. Write to stores only from services, read from stores only in components â€” no exceptions
   411â†’4. Normalize all external API data before writing to stores â€” components never parse raw responses
   412â†’5. Co-locate test files with source files using `.test.ts` suffix
   413â†’
   414â†’**Anti-Patterns (never do these):**
   415â†’- Creating a `utils/index.ts` barrel file â€” import directly from specific modules
   416â†’- Adding a UI framework "just for one component" â€” all islands use vanilla TS
   417â†’- Storing derived data in stores â€” compute in components (e.g., don't store formatted strings)
   418â†’- Using `setInterval` for REST polling â€” use recursive `setTimeout` to prevent drift on slow responses
   419â†’- Adding loading spinners or skeleton screens â€” the empty card with labels IS the loading state
   420â†’

   487â†’### Architectural Boundaries
   488â†’
   489â†’**External API Boundaries:**
   490â†’
   491â†’| Boundary | Service | Protocol | Direction |
   492â†’|---|---|---|---|
   493â†’| Binance | `connection-manager.ts` | WebSocket (`wss://`) | Inbound stream |
   494â†’| CoinCap | `connection-manager.ts` | WebSocket (`wss://`) | Inbound stream (fallback) |
   495â†’| CoinGecko | `market-data-service.ts` | REST (`https://`) | Outbound polling (60s) |
   496â†’
   497â†’All external communication is isolated in `src/lib/services/`. No component ever makes a network request directly.
   498â†’
   499â†’**Component Boundaries:**
   500â†’
   501â†’```
   502â†’Layout.astro (static shell)

   505â†’â”œâ”€â”€ MarketCapCard.astro (island, client:load)
   506â†’â”‚   â””â”€â”€ reads: marketStore
   507â†’â”œâ”€â”€ ChartCard.astro (island, client:load)
   508â†’â”‚   â””â”€â”€ reads: chartStore
   509â†’â”‚   â””â”€â”€ imports: theme-constants.ts, lightweight-charts
   510â†’â””â”€â”€ StatusIndicator.astro (island, client:load)
   511â†’    â””â”€â”€ reads: statusStore
   512â†’```
   513â†’
   514â†’**Data Boundary:**
   515â†’- No persistent storage â€” all data is in-memory Nano Stores
   516â†’- Stores are the single source of truth between services and components
   517â†’- Services write, components read â€” never the reverse

   539â†’| Animation standards | `global.css` (keyframes, transitions, prefers-reduced-motion) |
   540â†’
   541â†’### Integration Points
   542â†’
   543â†’**Internal Communication:**
   544â†’All inter-component communication flows through Nano Stores. No direct imports between components. No DOM events for data passing. The store is the only integration point.
   545â†’
   546â†’**External Integrations:**
   547â†’
   548â†’| Integration | Endpoint | Rate | Error Strategy |
   549â†’|---|---|---|---|
   550â†’| Binance WS | `wss://stream.binance.com:9443/ws/btcusdt@trade` | Continuous | Fallback to CoinCap |
   551â†’| CoinCap WS | `wss://ws.coincap.io/prices?assets=bitcoin` | Continuous | Fallback to REST polling |
   552â†’| CoinGecko REST | `https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/...` | Every 60s | Retain last known data |
   553â†’
   554â†’**Data Flow:**
   555â†’```
   556â†’[Binance WS] â”€â”€â†’ connection-manager.ts â”€â”€â†’ normalize â”€â”€â†’ priceStore â”€â”€â†’ PriceCard (DOM update)
   557â†’                                                                      â”€â”€â†’ Layout (tab title)
   558â†’                                        â”€â”€â†’ statusStore â”€â”€â†’ StatusIndicator (DOM update)
   559â†’                                                         â”€â”€â†’ PriceCard (stale badge)
   560â†’
   561â†’[CoinGecko REST] â”€â”€â†’ market-data-service.ts â”€â”€â†’ marketStore â”€â”€â†’ MarketCapCard (DOM update)
   562â†’                                             â”€â”€â†’ chartStore  â”€â”€â†’ ChartCard (chart update)
   563â†’```
   564â†’
   565â†’### Development Workflow
   566â†’
   567â†’**Dev Server:** `npm run dev` â€” Astro dev server on `localhost:4321`, HMR for all files
   568â†’**Unit Tests:** `npm run test` â€” Vitest, runs co-located `.test.ts` files
   569â†’**E2E Test:** `npx playwright test` â€” single smoke test: page loads, price element appears
   570â†’**Build:** `npm run build` â€” outputs static files to `dist/`
   571â†’**Preview:** `npm run preview` â€” serves `dist/` locally to verify production build
   572â†’
   573â†’## Architecture Validation Results
   574â†’
   575â†’### Coherence Validation âœ…
   576â†’
   577â†’**Decision Compatibility:**
   578â†’All technology choices are compatible and version-verified:
   579â†’- Astro 6.x + Tailwind CSS v4 (`@tailwindcss/vite`) â€” officially supported since Astro 5.2+
   580â†’- Nano Stores + Astro vanilla TS islands â€” Astro's recommended inter-island communication
   581â†’- lightweight-charts + vanilla TS â€” framework-agnostic imperative API, no wrapper needed
   582â†’- Vitest + Astro â€” recommended by Astro docs
   583â†’- TypeScript strict mode â€” no conflicts with any dependency
   584â†’
   585â†’No contradictory decisions found.
   586â†’
   587â†’**Pattern Consistency:**
   588â†’- Naming conventions align with Astro ecosystem norms (PascalCase components, kebab-case modules)
   589â†’- Unidirectional data flow (Service â†’ Store â†’ Component â†’ DOM) consistently enforced across all patterns
   590â†’- Error handling approach (silent recovery, stale indicators) consistent with UX spec's "never show error UI" principle
   591â†’- Store patterns (null init, typed generics, service-only writes) applied uniformly
   592â†’
   593â†’**Structure Alignment:**
   594â†’- Every architectural decision maps to a specific file in the project tree
   595â†’- Boundaries are clean: services, stores, components, and utils each have dedicated directories
   596â†’- No orphan files or ambiguous ownership
   597â†’
   598â†’### Requirements Coverage Validation âœ…
   599â†’
   600â†’**Functional Requirements Coverage:**
   601â†’All 23 FRs (FR1â€“FR23) are fully covered by architectural decisions. Each FR maps to specific files and services as documented in the Requirements to Structure Mapping.
   602â†’
   603â†’**Non-Functional Requirements Coverage:**
   604â†’16 of 17 NFRs fully covered. NFR17 (no memory leaks over 24h+) required additional specification â€” addressed below in Gap Analysis.
   605â†’
   606â†’### Implementation Readiness Validation âœ…
   607â†’
   608â†’**Decision Completeness:**
   609â†’- All critical and important decisions documented with specific versions
   610â†’- Technology stack fully specified with initialization commands
   611â†’- No ambiguous "TBD" decisions remaining for v1
   612â†’
   613â†’**Structure Completeness:**
   614â†’- Complete project tree with every file named and purposed
   615â†’- All integration points explicitly mapped
   616â†’- Component boundaries defined with store read/write permissions
   617â†’
   618â†’**Pattern Completeness:**
   619â†’- 12 conflict points identified and resolved with mandatory patterns
   620â†’- Concrete code examples provided for store definitions and data flow
   621â†’- Anti-patterns explicitly listed to prevent common mistakes
   622â†’
   623â†’### Gap Analysis Results
   624â†’
   625â†’**Gap Found: NFR17 â€” Memory Leak Prevention (24h+ Sessions)**
   626â†’
   627â†’Priority: Important â€” addressed below.
   628â†’
   629â†’The architecture did not explicitly specify memory management for long-running sessions. Three leak vectors identified and resolved:
   630â†’
   631â†’**1. WebSocket listener accumulation:**
   632â†’- Risk: Each reconnect cycle could add new `onmessage`/`onerror` listeners without removing old ones
   633â†’- Rule: ConnectionManager MUST call `ws.close()` and null the reference before creating a new WebSocket instance. Never add listeners to a WebSocket that's being replaced.
   634â†’
   635â†’**2. Chart data array growth:**
   636â†’- Risk: `chartStore` array grows unbounded as new hourly data points arrive
   637â†’- Rule: Cap `chartStore` at 24 entries (24 hours of hourly data). When appending a new point, shift the oldest. Never let the array exceed the 24h window.
   638â†’
   639â†’**3. Timer reference leaks:**
   640â†’- Risk: `setTimeout` references from REST polling or reconnection backoff could accumulate if not properly cleared
   641â†’- Rule: Store all timer IDs. Clear previous timer before scheduling a new one. Use a single `AbortController` per service for coordinated cleanup if the page is ever torn down.
   642â†’
   643â†’**No other gaps found.** All critical, important, and nice-to-have areas are covered.
   644â†’
   645â†’### Architecture Completeness Checklist
   646â†’
   647â†’**âœ… Requirements Analysis**
   648â†’- [x] Project context thoroughly analyzed
   649â†’- [x] Scale and complexity assessed (low)
   650â†’- [x] Technical constraints identified (Astro, Tailwind, no backend)
   651â†’- [x] Cross-cutting concerns mapped (5 concerns)
   652â†’
   653â†’**âœ… Starter Template**
   654â†’- [x] Technology domain identified (frontend web)
   655â†’- [x] Starter options evaluated with rationale
   656â†’- [x] Initialization commands documented with current versions
   657â†’- [x] Tailwind v4 token strategy decided
   658â†’
   659â†’**âœ… Architectural Decisions**
   660â†’- [x] Critical decisions documented (Nano Stores, vanilla TS, WebSocket manager)
   661â†’- [x] Technology stack fully specified with versions
   662â†’- [x] Data interfaces defined (PriceTick, MarketData, ChartPoint, ConnectionStatus)
   663â†’- [x] Data flow architecture mapped
   664â†’- [x] Error handling strategy defined
   665â†’- [x] Memory management rules specified
   666â†’
   667â†’**âœ… Implementation Patterns**
   668â†’- [x] Naming conventions established (files, code, CSS)
   669â†’- [x] Structure patterns defined with project tree
   670â†’- [x] Store patterns specified with code examples
   671â†’- [x] Communication patterns documented (unidirectional flow)
   672â†’- [x] Process patterns complete (error handling, init sequence, animations)
   673â†’- [x] Anti-patterns explicitly listed
   674â†’
   675â†’**âœ… Project Structure**
   676â†’- [x] Complete directory structure defined
   677â†’- [x] Component boundaries established with store permissions
   678â†’- [x] Integration points mapped (3 external APIs)
   679â†’- [x] Requirements to structure mapping complete (all 23 FRs)
   680â†’- [x] Development workflow documented
   681â†’
   682â†’### Architecture Readiness Assessment
   683â†’
   684â†’**Overall Status:** READY FOR IMPLEMENTATION
   685â†’
   686â†’**Confidence Level:** High â€” low-complexity project with fully specified stack, complete patterns, and all requirements mapped.
   687â†’
   688â†’**Key Strengths:**
   689â†’- Tight scope â€” 1 page, 5 components, 4 stores, 2 services. Every file is named and purposed.
   690â†’- Clear boundaries â€” services write stores, components read stores. No ambiguity.
   691â†’- Specific and quantified â€” bundle budgets, timing thresholds, API endpoints, reconnection parameters all documented with exact values.
   692â†’- Anti-patterns documented â€” AI agents know what NOT to do, not just what to do.
   693â†’
   694â†’**Areas for Future Enhancement (Post-MVP):**
   695â†’- Deployment configuration (Phase 2 â€” Vercel/Netlify)
   696â†’- CI/CD pipeline with Lighthouse checks (Phase 2)
   697â†’- Component library extraction for reuse (Phase 3)
   698â†’- PWA service worker for offline support (Phase 2)
   699â†’
   700â†’### Implementation Handoff
   701â†’
   702â†’**AI Agent Guidelines:**
   703â†’- Follow all architectural decisions exactly as documented
   704â†’- Use implementation patterns consistently across all components
   705â†’- Respect project structure and boundaries â€” no alternative file organizations
   706â†’- Refer to this document for all architectural questions
   707â†’- When in doubt, check the anti-patterns list before proceeding
   708â†’
   709â†’**First Implementation Priority:**
   710â†’```bash
   711â†’npm create astro@latest -- --template minimal --typescript strict
   712â†’npx astro add tailwind
   713â†’npm install lightweight-charts @fontsource/jetbrains-mono @fontsource/inter nanostores
   714â†’npm install -D eslint prettier eslint-plugin-astro prettier-plugin-astro vitest
   715â†’```

# Architecture Decision Document

_This document builds collaboratively through step-by-step discovery. Sections are appended as we work through each architectural decision together._

## Project Context Analysis

### Requirements Overview

**Functional Requirements:**
23 FRs across 6 categories. The dominant category is Connection Resilience (FR11â€“FR16) â€” 6 requirements governing WebSocket lifecycle, multi-source fallback, and stale data handling. This is the most architecturally significant area despite the project's low overall complexity. The remaining FRs cover straightforward data display (price, change, market cap, chart) and visual presentation (dark theme, animations, responsive layout).

**Non-Functional Requirements:**
17 NFRs with specific quantified targets. Performance NFRs (1â€“8) define a tight bundle budget (<100KB JS, <50KB chart lib) and strict rendering metrics (CLS = 0, LCP <1.5s, 60 FPS). Integration NFRs (9â€“14) specify WebSocket connection behavior (500ms connect, exponential backoff 1sâ€“30s, 60s REST polling). Reliability NFRs (15â€“17) require graceful degradation and no memory leaks over 24h+ sessions.

**Scale & Complexity:**

- Primary domain: Frontend web â€” real-time data visualization
- Complexity level: Low
- Estimated architectural components: 5 UI components + 1 WebSocket service layer + 1 REST polling service + 1 shared theme constants module

### Technical Constraints & Dependencies

- **Framework locked:** Astro JS with Islands Architecture â€” non-negotiable
- **Styling locked:** Tailwind CSS with custom semantic tokens
- **Chart library:** lightweight-charts (TradingView) â€” chosen for finance-native API, ~40KB gzipped
- **External API dependencies (no auth required):**
  - Binance WebSocket (primary real-time) â€” 24h connection expiry, potential geo-restrictions
  - CoinCap WebSocket (fallback real-time) â€” simpler, universal access
  - CoinGecko REST (supplementary data) â€” 30 req/min free tier, 60s polling interval
- **No backend, no SSR, no routing, no SEO, no user data storage**
- **Browser targets:** Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge (latest 2 versions) â€” backdrop-filter supported across all

### Cross-Cutting Concerns Identified

1. **WebSocket connection state** â€” Shared across Price Card, Status Indicator, and fallback logic. Needs a unified connection manager that multiple islands can observe.
2. **Design token consistency** â€” Tailwind config tokens must stay in sync with lightweight-charts JS API colors. Solved via shared `theme-constants.ts` file.
3. **Stale data handling** â€” Affects Price Card (badge display), Status Indicator (state text), and tab title (should it show stale price?). Needs a consistent staleness threshold (>30s).
4. **Animation/motion standards** â€” Consistent timing (300ms/400ms/500ms), easing curves, and `prefers-reduced-motion` support across all components.
5. **Number formatting** â€” USD formatting with comma separators, abbreviated market cap, consistent decimal places across all data displays.

## Starter Template Evaluation

### Primary Technology Domain

Frontend web â€” single-page real-time dashboard built on Astro Islands Architecture.

### Starter Options Considered

| Option | Template | Fit | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| A | `create astro -- --template minimal` | Perfect â€” clean slate, zero cruft | **Selected** |
| B | Astroplate / AstroWind / Astroship | Poor â€” blog/content-site scaffolding to remove | Rejected |
| C | Manual setup | No advantage over CLI wizard | Rejected |

### Selected Starter: Astro Minimal + CLI Integrations

**Rationale:** The project has exactly 1 page, 5 components, and no content management. Any opinionated starter adds complexity to remove. The minimal template + `astro add` commands give us the exact stack specified in the PRD with zero waste.

**Initialization Commands:**

```bash
npm create astro@latest -- --template minimal --typescript strict
npx astro add tailwind
npm install lightweight-charts @fontsource/jetbrains-mono @fontsource/inter
npm install -D eslint prettier eslint-plugin-astro prettier-plugin-astro
npm install -D vitest
```

**Package manager:** npm (explicit choice â€” simplest for solo developer, no pnpm/yarn complexity needed).

### Architectural Decisions Provided by Starter

**Language & Runtime:**
- Astro 6.x (current stable 6.0.8) â€” requires Node 22+
- TypeScript in strict mode â€” catches type errors early, appropriate for intermediate developer
- Astro's built-in TypeScript support (no separate tsconfig needed beyond Astro defaults)
- Note: Astro 6 is a recent stable release. Headline features (redesigned dev server, CSP APIs, live content collections) aren't needed for this project â€” we're on Astro 6 because `create astro@latest` gives us Astro 6. Acceptable early-adopter risk for a personal project with no user data.

**Styling Solution:**
- Tailwind CSS v4 via `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin
- CSS-first configuration: `@theme` directives in `src/styles/global.css` replace `tailwind.config.js` for token definition
- Design tokens (`accent-primary`, `bg-base`, `text-muted`, etc.) defined as CSS custom properties via `@theme`
- **Tailwind v4 â†” lightweight-charts token strategy:** Colors needed by the lightweight-charts JS API cannot be read from CSS `@theme` at import time. For v1, duplicate color values in a `theme-constants.ts` file â€” acceptable sync risk for a solo developer with 5 color values. Refactor to runtime `getComputedStyle()` reads if a theme switcher is ever added.

**Build Tooling:**
- Vite (bundled with Astro) â€” HMR, tree-shaking, optimized production builds
- Astro's static output mode (`output: 'static'`) â€” generates pure HTML/CSS/JS files
- Automatic CSS purging via Tailwind v4

**Code Quality:**
- ESLint with `eslint-plugin-astro` for Astro-aware linting
- Prettier with `prettier-plugin-astro` for consistent formatting
- Configured from first commit â€” important if project becomes open-source in Phase 3

**Testing Framework:**
- Vitest included in dev dependencies from project init
- Recommended test strategy for v1: unit tests for WebSocket manager and number formatting utilities, single Playwright smoke test for "page loads, price appears"
- Note: if chart config uses runtime `getComputedStyle()` in future, CSS values won't be available in Node.js test environment â€” the duplicated `theme-constants.ts` approach keeps test imports trivial

**Code Organization:**
- `src/pages/` â€” single `index.astro` page
- `src/components/` â€” Astro components (islands)
- `src/layouts/` â€” page layout wrapper
- `src/styles/` â€” global CSS with Tailwind `@import` and `@theme` tokens
- `src/lib/` â€” shared utilities (`theme-constants.ts`, formatting helpers, WebSocket manager)

**Development Experience:**
- Astro dev server with HMR
- TypeScript type checking
- Tailwind CSS IntelliSense (VS Code extension)

**Note:** Project initialization using these commands should be the first implementation story.

## Core Architectural Decisions

### Decision Priority Analysis

**Critical Decisions (Block Implementation):**
1. Inter-island state management â€” Nano Stores
2. Island UI framework â€” Vanilla TypeScript (no framework)
3. WebSocket manager architecture â€” single manager, multiple consumers via stores

**Important Decisions (Shape Architecture):**
4. Message normalization â€” common `PriceTick` interface across data sources
5. Stale data threshold â€” 30 seconds

**Deferred Decisions (Post-MVP):**
- Hosting provider selection (Phase 2)
- CI/CD pipeline (Phase 2)
- PWA configuration (Phase 2)
- Component library extraction (Phase 3)

### Data Architecture

No database. No persistent storage. All data is ephemeral â€” sourced from external APIs and held in memory via Nano Stores.

**Data flow:**
```
Binance WebSocket â”€â”€â†’ ConnectionManager â”€â”€â†’ Nano Store (priceStore) â”€â”€â†’ Price Card island
CoinCap WebSocket â”€â”ک  (fallback)           Nano Store (statusStore) â”€â”€â†’ Status Indicator island
                                            Nano Store (priceStore) â”€â”€â†’ Tab title updater

CoinGecko REST â”€â”€â”€â”€â†’ MarketDataService â”€â”€â†’ Nano Store (marketStore) â”€â”€â†’ Market Cap Card island
                     (60s polling)         Nano Store (chartStore)  â”€â”€â†’ Chart Card island
```

**Data interfaces:**
- `PriceTick`: `{ price: number, timestamp: number, direction: 'up' | 'down' | 'neutral' }` â€” normalized from both Binance and CoinCap formats
- `MarketData`: `{ marketCap: number, change24h: number, changePercent24h: number, high24h: number, low24h: number }` â€” from CoinGecko REST
- `ChartPoint`: `{ time: number, value: number }` â€” hourly price points for lightweight-charts
- `ConnectionStatus`: `'connecting' | 'live' | 'reconnecting' | 'stale' | 'fallback'`

### Authentication & Security

Not applicable. No user accounts, no API keys, no sensitive data. All external APIs are public and keyless.

**Client-side security considerations:**
- No user input to sanitize (read-only dashboard)
- WebSocket connections use `wss://` (TLS encrypted)
- No localStorage/sessionStorage usage â€” no data persistence to protect
- Content Security Policy: consider adding CSP headers when deploying (Phase 2) to restrict WebSocket origins

### API & Communication Patterns

**WebSocket Manager (`src/lib/connection-manager.ts`):**
- Singleton pattern â€” one instance manages the entire WebSocket lifecycle
- Fallback chain: Binance `wss://stream.binance.com:9443/ws/btcusdt@trade` â†’ CoinCap `wss://ws.coincap.io/prices?assets=bitcoin`
- Reconnection: exponential backoff starting at 1s, doubling to max 30s cap
- 24h expiry: Binance hard-expires connections at 24 hours â€” handled as a standard reconnect cycle
- Stale threshold: 30 seconds without a price update â†’ `ConnectionStatus` transitions to `'stale'`
- Publishes normalized `PriceTick` to `priceStore` (Nano Store)
- Publishes `ConnectionStatus` to `statusStore` (Nano Store)

**REST Polling Service (`src/lib/market-data-service.ts`):**
- Polls CoinGecko REST API every 60 seconds (within 30 req/min free tier)
- Fetches: market cap, 24h change, 24h high/low, hourly historical data for chart
- On API failure: retains last known data, does not clear stores
- Publishes to `marketStore` and `chartStore` (Nano Stores)

**Error Handling Standards:**
- WebSocket errors â†’ silent reconnection via backoff, status indicator updates
- REST errors â†’ retain stale data, log to console, no user-facing error UI
- No thrown exceptions reaching the UI â€” all error states expressed via store values
- Console logging for debugging only â€” no external error reporting service in v1

### Frontend Architecture

**State Management: Nano Stores**
- Decision: Nano Stores (~1KB) as the reactive state layer shared across Astro islands
- Rationale: Astro's officially recommended approach for inter-island communication. Reactive, tiny, framework-agnostic.
- Stores:
  - `priceStore` â€” current `PriceTick` (written by ConnectionManager)
  - `statusStore` â€” current `ConnectionStatus` (written by ConnectionManager)
  - `marketStore` â€” current `MarketData` (written by MarketDataService)
  - `chartStore` â€” array of `ChartPoint` (written by MarketDataService)

**Island UI Framework: Vanilla TypeScript**
- Decision: No UI framework. Islands use Astro `<script>` tags with `client:load` for direct DOM updates.
- Rationale: Islands perform simple operations (update text, toggle classes, call lightweight-charts API). Framework overhead adds bundle weight without proportional value. Keeps total JS well within <100KB budget.

**Component Architecture:**
- 5 Astro components as defined in UX spec: Page Shell (static), Price Card (island), Market Cap Card (island), Chart Card (island), Status Indicator (island)
- Each island subscribes to relevant Nano Stores and updates DOM directly
- No component nesting beyond page â†’ island â€” flat hierarchy

**Bundle Optimization:**
- Astro static output â€” only island JS ships to client
- Nano Stores: ~1KB gzipped
- lightweight-charts: ~40KB gzipped
- @fontsource fonts: self-hosted, subset to Latin characters
- Estimated total hydrated JS: ~50-60KB gzipped (well within 100KB budget)

### Infrastructure & Deployment

**v1: Local Development Only**
- `npm run dev` â€” Astro dev server with HMR
- No deployment pipeline, no hosting configuration
- Environment configuration: API endpoints as hardcoded constants in `src/lib/` (all public, no secrets)

**Phase 2 Deployment Path (documented, not implemented):**
- Target: Vercel or Netlify (static hosting)
- Build: `npm run build` â†’ `dist/` directory with static HTML/CSS/JS
- No server-side requirements â€” pure static deployment
- Consider moving API endpoints to `.env` if multiple environments needed

### Decision Impact Analysis

**Implementation Sequence:**
1. Project initialization (starter commands)
2. Tailwind `@theme` token configuration + `theme-constants.ts`
3. Nano Stores setup (4 stores with TypeScript interfaces)
4. ConnectionManager (WebSocket lifecycle + fallback chain)
5. MarketDataService (REST polling)
6. Page Shell (static layout grid)
7. Price Card island (subscribes to priceStore + statusStore)
8. Status Indicator island (subscribes to statusStore)
9. Market Cap Card island (subscribes to marketStore)
10. Chart Card island (subscribes to chartStore, configures lightweight-charts)

**Cross-Component Dependencies:**
- ConnectionManager â†’ priceStore, statusStore (writes)
- MarketDataService â†’ marketStore, chartStore (writes)
- Price Card â†’ priceStore, statusStore (reads)
- Market Cap Card â†’ marketStore (reads)
- Chart Card â†’ chartStore (reads)
- Status Indicator â†’ statusStore (reads)
- theme-constants.ts â†’ Chart Card (color configuration)

## Implementation Patterns & Consistency Rules

### Critical Conflict Points Identified

12 areas where AI agents could make different choices, organized into 5 categories. All patterns below are mandatory for implementation consistency.

### Naming Patterns

**File Naming:**
- Astro components: PascalCase â€” `PriceCard.astro`, `ChartCard.astro`, `StatusIndicator.astro`, `PageShell.astro`
- TypeScript modules: kebab-case â€” `connection-manager.ts`, `market-data-service.ts`, `theme-constants.ts`
- Store files: kebab-case â€” `price-store.ts`, `status-store.ts`, `market-store.ts`, `chart-store.ts`
- Test files: co-located with source, `.test.ts` suffix â€” `connection-manager.test.ts`, `format-utils.test.ts`
- CSS: single global file â€” `src/styles/global.css`

**Code Naming:**
- Variables and functions: camelCase â€” `priceStore`, `formatCurrency()`, `handlePriceTick()`
- Types and interfaces: PascalCase â€” `PriceTick`, `MarketData`, `ConnectionStatus`
- Constants: UPPER_SNAKE_CASE â€” `STALE_THRESHOLD_MS`, `WS_BINANCE_URL`, `REST_POLL_INTERVAL_MS`
- Store names: camelCase with `Store` suffix â€” `priceStore`, `statusStore`, `marketStore`, `chartStore`
- Event handlers: `handle` + noun + verb pattern â€” `handlePriceUpdate()`, `handleConnectionDrop()`

**CSS/Tailwind Naming:**
- Semantic token names in `@theme`: kebab-case with category prefix â€” `--color-accent-primary`, `--color-bg-base`, `--color-text-muted`
- `@apply` compositions (max 3): `.card-glass`, `.price-text`, `.status-badge`
- Astro scoped style keyframe names: kebab-case â€” `@keyframes price-flash`, `@keyframes fade-in`

### Structure Patterns

**Project Organization:**
```
src/
â”œâ”€â”€ pages/
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ index.astro              # Single page entry
â”œâ”€â”€ layouts/
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ Layout.astro             # Page shell wrapper
â”œâ”€â”€ components/
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ PriceCard.astro          # Hydrated island
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ MarketCapCard.astro      # Hydrated island
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ ChartCard.astro          # Hydrated island
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ StatusIndicator.astro    # Hydrated island
â”œâ”€â”€ lib/
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ stores/
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ price-store.ts
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ status-store.ts
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ market-store.ts
â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ chart-store.ts
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ services/
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ connection-manager.ts
â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ market-data-service.ts
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ utils/
â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ format-utils.ts      # Number formatting helpers
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ theme-constants.ts       # Shared color values for lightweight-charts
â””â”€â”€ styles/
    â””â”€â”€ global.css               # Tailwind @import, @theme tokens, @apply compositions
```

**Rules:**
- Components in `src/components/` â€” flat, no subdirectories (only 4 components)
- Business logic in `src/lib/services/` â€” never in components
- Stores in `src/lib/stores/` â€” one file per store
- Utility functions in `src/lib/utils/` â€” pure functions only, no side effects
- Tests co-located: `src/lib/services/connection-manager.test.ts` next to source file

### Store Patterns

**Nano Store conventions:**

```typescript
// Store definition pattern (every store file follows this)
import { atom } from 'nanostores';
import type { PriceTick } from '../types';

export const priceStore = atom<PriceTick | null>(null);
```

**Rules:**
- All stores initialize to `null` (not undefined, not default objects)
- Only services write to stores â€” never components
- Components subscribe in `<script>` blocks using `store.subscribe()`
- One store per concern â€” never combine unrelated data in a single store
- Type every store with explicit generic: `atom<PriceTick | null>`

### Format Patterns

**Number Formatting (all handled by `format-utils.ts`):**
- Price: `$68,432.17` â€” USD, comma separators, always 2 decimal places
- Percentage: `+2.3%` or `-1.8%` â€” sign always shown, 1 decimal place
- Absolute change: `+$1,534.22` or `-$892.40` â€” sign + USD, 2 decimal places
- Market cap: `$1.34T` or `$892.45B` â€” abbreviated with 2 decimal places
- 24h range: `$66.9k â€” $68.9k` â€” abbreviated with 1 decimal place
- Chart tooltip: `$68,432.17 at 14:30` â€” full price + 24h time (no date, no seconds)

**Rules:**
- All formatting goes through `format-utils.ts` helper functions â€” never inline formatting in components
- Use `Intl.NumberFormat` as the base â€” no manual string building
- Direction indicators always pair symbol with color: `â†‘` green, `â†“` red, `â†’` white (neutral)
- Never use color alone to communicate direction

### Communication Patterns

**Store update flow (unidirectional):**
```
External API â†’ Service â†’ Store â†’ Component â†’ DOM
```

**Rules:**
- Services are the only writers to stores
- Components are read-only subscribers â€” never call `store.set()` from a component
- No component-to-component communication â€” always go through stores
- No direct DOM manipulation outside of store subscription callbacks in island `<script>` blocks

**WebSocket message handling:**
- Raw messages are parsed and normalized in `connection-manager.ts` â€” components never see raw WebSocket data
- Normalization happens before store write â€” stores only contain clean `PriceTick` objects
- Binance format: `{ p: "68432.17", ... }` â†’ `{ price: 68432.17, timestamp: Date.now(), direction: 'up' }`
- CoinCap format: `{ bitcoin: "68432.17" }` â†’ same normalized `PriceTick`

### Process Patterns

**Error Handling:**
- WebSocket errors: catch silently, update `statusStore` to `'reconnecting'`, trigger backoff â€” no console.error for expected disconnects
- REST errors: catch silently, retain last store values, `console.warn()` with endpoint and status code
- Parsing errors (malformed API response): `console.error()` with raw payload, skip the update, do not crash the island
- Never throw from a store subscription callback â€” wrap in try/catch
- Never show error UI to the user â€” the UX spec explicitly avoids error states. Use stale indicators instead.

**Initialization sequence:**
- Services initialize in `Layout.astro` via a single `<script>` block that imports and starts `ConnectionManager` and `MarketDataService`
- Islands hydrate via `client:load` and immediately subscribe to relevant stores
- Order doesn't matter â€” stores start as `null`, components render empty state, data appears when stores update

**Animation implementation:**
- All durations as constants in a shared location (CSS custom properties or `theme-constants.ts`)
- Price flash: CSS transition on `color` property, triggered by toggling a data attribute (`data-flash="up"`)
- Entry fade-in: CSS transition on `opacity`, triggered by removing a `data-loading` attribute
- Chart draw: lightweight-charts built-in animation API
- Always check `prefers-reduced-motion` â€” if enabled, set all durations to 0ms

### Enforcement Guidelines

**All AI Agents MUST:**
1. Follow the file naming and project structure patterns exactly â€” no alternative organizations
2. Use `format-utils.ts` for all number display â€” no inline `toFixed()` or manual formatting
3. Write to stores only from services, read from stores only in components â€” no exceptions
4. Normalize all external API data before writing to stores â€” components never parse raw responses
5. Co-locate test files with source files using `.test.ts` suffix

**Anti-Patterns (never do these):**
- Creating a `utils/index.ts` barrel file â€” import directly from specific modules
- Adding a UI framework "just for one component" â€” all islands use vanilla TS
- Storing derived data in stores â€” compute in components (e.g., don't store formatted strings)
- Using `setInterval` for REST polling â€” use recursive `setTimeout` to prevent drift on slow responses
- Adding loading spinners or skeleton screens â€” the empty card with labels IS the loading state

### File Purposes

**Root Configuration:**
- `astro.config.mjs` â€” Astro config with `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin, `output: 'static'`
- `tsconfig.json` â€” extends Astro's base TypeScript config, strict mode
- `eslint.config.js` â€” ESLint flat config with `eslint-plugin-astro`

- `vitest.config.ts` â€” Vitest config for unit tests
- `playwright.config.ts` â€” Playwright config for e2e smoke test

**Source Entry Points:**

- `src/layouts/Layout.astro` â€” page shell (sets `<html>` bg color, loads fonts, initializes services via `<script>`, defines CSS grid)

**Types:**
- `src/lib/types.ts` â€” all shared TypeScript interfaces: `PriceTick`, `MarketData`, `ChartPoint`, `ConnectionStatus`

### Architectural Boundaries

**External API Boundaries:**

| Binance | `connection-manager.ts` | WebSocket (`wss://`) | Inbound stream |
| CoinCap | `connection-manager.ts` | WebSocket (`wss://`) | Inbound stream (fallback) |
| CoinGecko | `market-data-service.ts` | REST (`https://`) | Outbound polling (60s) |

All external communication is isolated in `src/lib/services/`. No component ever makes a network request directly.

**Component Boundaries:**

```

```

**Data Boundary:**
- No persistent storage â€” all data is in-memory Nano Stores
- Stores are the single source of truth between services and components
- Services write, components read â€” never the reverse

### Requirements to Structure Mapping

**FR Category â†’ Files:**

| Real-Time Price Display (FR1â€“FR3) | `PriceCard.astro` | `connection-manager.ts`, `price-store.ts`, `format-utils.ts` |

**Cross-Cutting Concerns â†’ Files:**

### Integration Points

**Internal Communication:**
All inter-component communication flows through Nano Stores. No direct imports between components. No DOM events for data passing. The store is the only integration point.

**External Integrations:**

| CoinCap WS | `wss://ws.coincap.io/prices?assets=bitcoin` | Continuous | Fallback to REST polling |
| CoinGecko REST | `https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/...` | Every 60s | Retain last known data |

**Data Flow:**

## Architecture Validation Results

### Coherence Validation âœ…

**Decision Compatibility:**
All technology choices are compatible and version-verified:
- Astro 6.x + Tailwind CSS v4 (`@tailwindcss/vite`) â€” officially supported since Astro 5.2+
- Nano Stores + Astro vanilla TS islands â€” Astro's recommended inter-island communication
- lightweight-charts + vanilla TS â€” framework-agnostic imperative API, no wrapper needed
- Vitest + Astro â€” recommended by Astro docs
- TypeScript strict mode â€” no conflicts with any dependency

No contradictory decisions found.

**Pattern Consistency:**
- Naming conventions align with Astro ecosystem norms (PascalCase components, kebab-case modules)
- Unidirectional data flow (Service â†’ Store â†’ Component â†’ DOM) consistently enforced across all patterns
- Error handling approach (silent recovery, stale indicators) consistent with UX spec's "never show error UI" principle
- Store patterns (null init, typed generics, service-only writes) applied uniformly

**Structure Alignment:**
- Every architectural decision maps to a specific file in the project tree
- Boundaries are clean: services, stores, components, and utils each have dedicated directories
- No orphan files or ambiguous ownership

### Requirements Coverage Validation âœ…

**Functional Requirements Coverage:**
All 23 FRs (FR1â€“FR23) are fully covered by architectural decisions. Each FR maps to specific files and services as documented in the Requirements to Structure Mapping.

**Non-Functional Requirements Coverage:**
16 of 17 NFRs fully covered. NFR17 (no memory leaks over 24h+) required additional specification â€” addressed below in Gap Analysis.

### Implementation Readiness Validation âœ…

**Decision Completeness:**
- All critical and important decisions documented with specific versions
- Technology stack fully specified with initialization commands
- No ambiguous "TBD" decisions remaining for v1

**Structure Completeness:**
- Complete project tree with every file named and purposed
- All integration points explicitly mapped
- Component boundaries defined with store read/write permissions

**Pattern Completeness:**
- 12 conflict points identified and resolved with mandatory patterns
- Concrete code examples provided for store definitions and data flow
- Anti-patterns explicitly listed to prevent common mistakes

### Gap Analysis Results

**Gap Found: NFR17 â€” Memory Leak Prevention (24h+ Sessions)**

Priority: Important â€” addressed below.

The architecture did not explicitly specify memory management for long-running sessions. Three leak vectors identified and resolved:

**1. WebSocket listener accumulation:**
- Risk: Each reconnect cycle could add new `onmessage`/`onerror` listeners without removing old ones
- Rule: ConnectionManager MUST call `ws.close()` and null the reference before creating a new WebSocket instance. Never add listeners to a WebSocket that's being replaced.

**2. Chart data array growth:**
- Risk: `chartStore` array grows unbounded as new hourly data points arrive
- Rule: Cap `chartStore` at 24 entries (24 hours of hourly data). When appending a new point, shift the oldest. Never let the array exceed the 24h window.

**3. Timer reference leaks:**
- Risk: `setTimeout` references from REST polling or reconnection backoff could accumulate if not properly cleared
- Rule: Store all timer IDs. Clear previous timer before scheduling a new one. Use a single `AbortController` per service for coordinated cleanup if the page is ever torn down.

**No other gaps found.** All critical, important, and nice-to-have areas are covered.

### Architecture Readiness Assessment

**Overall Status:** READY FOR IMPLEMENTATION

**Confidence Level:** High â€” low-complexity project with fully specified stack, complete patterns, and all requirements mapped.

**Key Strengths:**
- Tight scope â€” 1 page, 5 components, 4 stores, 2 services. Every file is named and purposed.
- Clear boundaries â€” services write stores, components read stores. No ambiguity.
- Specific and quantified â€” bundle budgets, timing thresholds, API endpoints, reconnection parameters all documented with exact values.
- Anti-patterns documented â€” AI agents know what NOT to do, not just what to do.

**Areas for Future Enhancement (Post-MVP):**
- Deployment configuration (Phase 2 â€” Vercel/Netlify)
- CI/CD pipeline with Lighthouse checks (Phase 2)
- Component library extraction for reuse (Phase 3)
- PWA service worker for offline support (Phase 2)

### Implementation Handoff

**AI Agent Guidelines:**
- Follow all architectural decisions exactly as documented
- Use implementation patterns consistently across all components
- Respect project structure and boundaries â€” no alternative file organizations
- Refer to this document for all architectural questions
- When in doubt, check the anti-patterns list before proceeding

    19â†’# Product Requirements Document - Metrics
    20â†’
    21â†’**Author:** Dmytro
    22â†’**Date:** 2026-03-25
    23â†’
    24â†’## Executive Summary
    25â†’
    26â†’Metrics is a single-page Bitcoin dashboard built on Astro JS that delivers real-time BTC price data through a polished, dark crypto interface. It solves a specific problem: checking the Bitcoin price today means navigating bloated exchange platforms or news aggregators packed with irrelevant coins, ads, and trading interfaces. Metrics provides the answer in one glance â€” live price via WebSocket, 24-hour change, market cap, and a sparkline chart â€” with zero accounts, zero configuration, and zero noise.
    27â†’
    28â†’The target user is a developer (initially Dmytro) who wants a fast, beautiful, always-open BTC tab. The product is designed around three pillars: **speed** (sub-2s page load, sub-1s price updates), **aesthetics** (dark crypto visual identity with glassmorphism, neon accents, and monospaced typography), and **focus** (one asset, one page, one purpose).
    29â†’
    30â†’Deliberate constraint is the feature. Where CoinGecko, CoinMarketCap, and exchange UIs compete on breadth, Metrics competes on depth of simplicity. The core insight: the best dashboard is the one you don't have to think about. It's built for someone who wants to *know* the price, not *trade* it. Astro's Islands Architecture hydrates only the interactive components (price ticker, chart) while the shell stays static HTML â€” a page that loads like a static site but updates like a live app.
    31â†’
    32â†’## Project Classification
    33â†’
    34â†’- **Project Type:** Web application (SPA-like with Astro Islands Architecture)
    35â†’- **Domain:** Fintech (cryptocurrency data visualization)
    36â†’- **Complexity:** Low â€” read-only public API consumption, no user data, no transactions, no regulatory requirements
    37â†’- **Project Context:** Greenfield â€” new product, no existing codebase
    38â†’
    39â†’## Success Criteria
    40â†’
    41â†’### User Success
    42â†’
    43â†’- **Instant awareness:** User opens a tab and knows the current BTC price within 1 second â€” no scanning, no clicking, no waiting
    44â†’- **Pinned tab worthy:** The dashboard is reliable and beautiful enough to earn a permanent spot in the browser tab bar
    45â†’- **Zero learning curve:** No onboarding, no configuration, no accounts â€” it just works on first visit
    46â†’
    47â†’### Business Success
    48â†’
    49â†’- **Personal utility:** Dmytro uses Metrics daily as his primary BTC price check, replacing CoinGecko/Google
    50â†’- **Craft quality:** The project demonstrates polished frontend engineering â€” suitable for sharing as open-source or a portfolio piece
    51â†’- **Deployable:** Can be moved from local dev to public hosting (Vercel/Netlify) with minimal effort when ready
    52â†’
    53â†’### Technical Success
    54â†’
    55â†’Specific targets defined in Non-Functional Requirements (NFR1â€“NFR17). Key benchmarks:
    56â†’
    57â†’- Lighthouse performance â‰¥ 90, accessibility â‰¥ 80
    58â†’- WebSocket connection uptime > 99% during active browser sessions
    59â†’- Total JS bundle (hydrated islands only) < 100KB gzipped
    60â†’- Chart library < 50KB gzipped
    61â†’
    62â†’## User Journeys
    63â†’
    64â†’### Journey 1: The Morning Price Check (Happy Path)
    65â†’
    66â†’**Dmytro, developer, morning coffee ritual.**
    67â†’
    68â†’It's 8:15 AM. Dmytro grabs his coffee and opens his laptop. Among his pinned browser tabs, Metrics is already there. He glances at it â€” **$68,432.17**, green arrow, **+2.3% in 24h**. The sparkline shows a gentle upward trend since yesterday afternoon. Market cap sits at $1.34T. He knows everything he needs to know in under 2 seconds. He moves on to his work. No clicks, no scrolling, no cognitive load. The tab title even shows the price, so sometimes he doesn't even need to switch to it.
    69â†’
    70â†’**Capabilities revealed:** Real-time price display, 24h change indicator, sparkline chart, market cap, tab title with price, instant page load from pinned tab state.
    71â†’
    72â†’### Journey 2: The Volatile Day (Edge Case â€” Reconnection & Stale Data)
    73â†’
    74â†’**Dmytro, mid-afternoon, market is moving fast.**
    75â†’
    76â†’Bitcoin just dropped 8% in an hour. Dmytro switches to his Metrics tab to watch it move. The price is ticking down live â€” **$62,100... $61,980... $61,850**. Then his WiFi blips. A subtle indicator appears: **"Reconnecting..."** in muted text. Within seconds, the WebSocket reconnects via exponential backoff and the live feed resumes. He didn't have to refresh. If the connection had stayed down longer, the price would have shown a stale-data badge so he'd know not to trust it, and the app would have fallen back to REST polling for updates every 60 seconds.
    77â†’
    78â†’**Capabilities revealed:** WebSocket reconnection with exponential backoff, stale data visual indicator, REST polling fallback, high-frequency price update rendering without layout shift.
    79â†’
    80â†’### Journey 3: The Fresh Visit (First-Time Load)
    81â†’
    82â†’**Dmytro opens Metrics for the first time on a new machine.**
    83â†’
    84â†’He types the local dev URL into his browser. The page loads in under 1.5 seconds â€” dark background fills the screen first (no flash of white), then the static shell renders immediately: labels, card outlines, layout. Within 500ms, the WebSocket connects and the price appears with a smooth fade-in. The sparkline draws itself with a quick animation. No loading spinners, no skeleton screens â€” just a fast, elegant entrance. He bookmarks it, pins the tab, and it becomes part of his daily setup.
    85â†’
    86â†’**Capabilities revealed:** Fast initial page load (static-first Astro), WebSocket connection initialization, graceful data hydration without layout shift, dark theme renders immediately (no FOUC), smooth entry animations.
    87â†’
    88â†’### Journey 4: The Mobile Glance (Responsive Edge Case)
    89â†’
    90â†’**Dmytro, on his phone, quick check while out.**
    91â†’
    92â†’He opens Metrics on his phone browser. The layout stacks vertically â€” price prominently on top, 24h change below, then the sparkline chart fills the width, market cap at the bottom. Everything is readable, touch-friendly, and the dark theme looks sharp on OLED. The WebSocket connects and the price starts updating live. Same experience, smaller screen.

# Product Requirements Document - Metrics

**Author:** Dmytro
**Date:** 2026-03-25

## Executive Summary

Metrics is a single-page Bitcoin dashboard built on Astro JS that delivers real-time BTC price data through a polished, dark crypto interface. It solves a specific problem: checking the Bitcoin price today means navigating bloated exchange platforms or news aggregators packed with irrelevant coins, ads, and trading interfaces. Metrics provides the answer in one glance â€” live price via WebSocket, 24-hour change, market cap, and a sparkline chart â€” with zero accounts, zero configuration, and zero noise.

The target user is a developer (initially Dmytro) who wants a fast, beautiful, always-open BTC tab. The product is designed around three pillars: **speed** (sub-2s page load, sub-1s price updates), **aesthetics** (dark crypto visual identity with glassmorphism, neon accents, and monospaced typography), and **focus** (one asset, one page, one purpose).

Deliberate constraint is the feature. Where CoinGecko, CoinMarketCap, and exchange UIs compete on breadth, Metrics competes on depth of simplicity. The core insight: the best dashboard is the one you don't have to think about. It's built for someone who wants to *know* the price, not *trade* it. Astro's Islands Architecture hydrates only the interactive components (price ticker, chart) while the shell stays static HTML â€” a page that loads like a static site but updates like a live app.

## Project Classification

- **Project Type:** Web application (SPA-like with Astro Islands Architecture)
- **Domain:** Fintech (cryptocurrency data visualization)
- **Complexity:** Low â€” read-only public API consumption, no user data, no transactions, no regulatory requirements
- **Project Context:** Greenfield â€” new product, no existing codebase

## Success Criteria

### User Success

- **Instant awareness:** User opens a tab and knows the current BTC price within 1 second â€” no scanning, no clicking, no waiting
- **Pinned tab worthy:** The dashboard is reliable and beautiful enough to earn a permanent spot in the browser tab bar
- **Zero learning curve:** No onboarding, no configuration, no accounts â€” it just works on first visit

### Business Success

- **Personal utility:** Dmytro uses Metrics daily as his primary BTC price check, replacing CoinGecko/Google
- **Craft quality:** The project demonstrates polished frontend engineering â€” suitable for sharing as open-source or a portfolio piece
- **Deployable:** Can be moved from local dev to public hosting (Vercel/Netlify) with minimal effort when ready

### Technical Success

Specific targets defined in Non-Functional Requirements (NFR1â€“NFR17). Key benchmarks:

- Lighthouse performance â‰¥ 90, accessibility â‰¥ 80
- WebSocket connection uptime > 99% during active browser sessions
- Total JS bundle (hydrated islands only) < 100KB gzipped
- Chart library < 50KB gzipped

## User Journeys

### Journey 1: The Morning Price Check (Happy Path)

**Dmytro, developer, morning coffee ritual.**

It's 8:15 AM. Dmytro grabs his coffee and opens his laptop. Among his pinned browser tabs, Metrics is already there. He glances at it â€” **$68,432.17**, green arrow, **+2.3% in 24h**. The sparkline shows a gentle upward trend since yesterday afternoon. Market cap sits at $1.34T. He knows everything he needs to know in under 2 seconds. He moves on to his work. No clicks, no scrolling, no cognitive load. The tab title even shows the price, so sometimes he doesn't even need to switch to it.

**Capabilities revealed:** Real-time price display, 24h change indicator, sparkline chart, market cap, tab title with price, instant page load from pinned tab state.

### Journey 2: The Volatile Day (Edge Case â€” Reconnection & Stale Data)

**Dmytro, mid-afternoon, market is moving fast.**

Bitcoin just dropped 8% in an hour. Dmytro switches to his Metrics tab to watch it move. The price is ticking down live â€” **$62,100... $61,980... $61,850**. Then his WiFi blips. A subtle indicator appears: **"Reconnecting..."** in muted text. Within seconds, the WebSocket reconnects via exponential backoff and the live feed resumes. He didn't have to refresh. If the connection had stayed down longer, the price would have shown a stale-data badge so he'd know not to trust it, and the app would have fallen back to REST polling for updates every 60 seconds.

**Capabilities revealed:** WebSocket reconnection with exponential backoff, stale data visual indicator, REST polling fallback, high-frequency price update rendering without layout shift.

### Journey 3: The Fresh Visit (First-Time Load)

**Dmytro opens Metrics for the first time on a new machine.**

He types the local dev URL into his browser. The page loads in under 1.5 seconds â€” dark background fills the screen first (no flash of white), then the static shell renders immediately: labels, card outlines, layout. Within 500ms, the WebSocket connects and the price appears with a smooth fade-in. The sparkline draws itself with a quick animation. No loading spinners, no skeleton screens â€” just a fast, elegant entrance. He bookmarks it, pins the tab, and it becomes part of his daily setup.

**Capabilities revealed:** Fast initial page load (static-first Astro), WebSocket connection initialization, graceful data hydration without layout shift, dark theme renders immediately (no FOUC), smooth entry animations.

### Journey 4: The Mobile Glance (Responsive Edge Case)

**Dmytro, on his phone, quick check while out.**

He opens Metrics on his phone browser. The layout stacks vertically â€” price prominently on top, 24h change below, then the sparkline chart fills the width, market cap at the bottom. Everything is readable, touch-friendly, and the dark theme looks sharp on OLED. The WebSocket connects and the price starts updating live. Same experience, smaller screen.

**Capabilities revealed:** Responsive design (desktop-first with mobile stacking), touch-friendly layout, OLED-friendly dark theme, full functionality on mobile browsers.

### Journey Requirements Summary

| Capability | J1 | J2 | J3 | J4 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Live BTC price via WebSocket | âœ“ | âœ“ | âœ“ | âœ“ |
| 24h change (% and absolute) | âœ“ | âœ“ | âœ“ | âœ“ |
| Market cap display | âœ“ | âœ“ | âœ“ | âœ“ |
| 24h sparkline chart | âœ“ | âœ“ | âœ“ | âœ“ |
| Tab title with price | âœ“ | | | |
| WebSocket reconnection | | âœ“ | | |
| Stale data indicator | | âœ“ | | |
| REST polling fallback | | âœ“ | | |
| Fast initial load (< 2s) | | | âœ“ | |
| Graceful data hydration | | | âœ“ | |
| No FOUC (dark theme first) | | | âœ“ | |
| Entry animations | | | âœ“ | |
| Responsive mobile layout | | | | âœ“ |

## Technical Architecture

### Astro Islands Architecture

Single-page Astro JS application. The page shell is static HTML (MPA pattern) with two hydrated client islands: the WebSocket price ticker and the sparkline chart. This gives the performance benefits of a static site with the interactivity of a live dashboard.

- **Rendering model:** Astro static-first with `client:load` directives for interactive islands (price ticker, chart component)
- **Real-time data:** WebSocket connections managed within hydrated islands only â€” no SSR or server-side WebSocket handling
- **State management:** Minimal â€” component-local state within each island. No global state store needed. Price data flows: WebSocket â†’ island component â†’ DOM
- **Asset strategy:** Tailwind CSS compiled at build time; JetBrains Mono and Inter loaded via `@fontsource` packages or self-hosted for performance

### Browser Support

- Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge (latest 2 versions each)
- No IE11 or legacy browser support
- `backdrop-filter` (glassmorphism) supported across all targets â€” no fallback needed

### Accessibility

- Semantic HTML structure (headings, landmarks, ARIA labels where meaningful)
- Sufficient color contrast for price text against dark backgrounds
- No formal WCAG compliance target â€” best-effort with sensible defaults

### Implementation Constraints

- **No SEO requirements** â€” personal dashboard, not indexed
- **No SSR needed** â€” fully static build with client-side hydration
- **No routing** â€” single page, no client-side router
- **No backend** â€” all data sourced from public WebSocket and REST APIs client-side
- **Dev server:** Astro dev server for local development; production build outputs static files

## Project Scoping & Phased Development

### MVP Strategy

**MVP Approach:** Experience MVP â€” the minimum feature set that delivers the full "open tab, know the price" experience with visual polish. No feature stubs or placeholder UI. Everything shipped in v1 works completely and looks finished.

**Resource Requirements:** Solo developer (Dmytro). Astro JS, Tailwind CSS, and TypeScript skills. No backend or DevOps needed for v1.

**Core User Journeys Supported:** All four (J1â€“J4) â€” full support.

### MVP Feature Set (Phase 1)

- Live BTC/USD price via Binance WebSocket with CoinCap fallback
- 24h price change (percentage and absolute) via CoinGecko REST
- Market cap display via CoinGecko REST (polled every 60s)
- 24h sparkline chart using lightweight-charts (TradingView), hourly data, hover tooltips
- Dark crypto aesthetic: near-black backgrounds, neon accents, glassmorphism cards, JetBrains Mono / Inter typography
- Tab title showing current price
- WebSocket reconnection with exponential backoff
- Stale data indicator on disconnect
- REST polling fallback when WebSocket is unavailable
- Responsive layout (desktop-first, mobile stacking)
- No FOUC â€” dark theme renders immediately
- Smooth entry animations for data hydration
- Local development setup with Astro dev server

### Key Technical Decisions

- **Chart library:** lightweight-charts (TradingView) â€” finance-native, ~40KB gzipped, fits bundle constraint
- **WebSocket fallback strategy:** Try Binance first, fall back to CoinCap on failure. No auto-detection or geo-check â€” simple sequential fallback acceptable for v1
- **Binance 24h expiry:** Reconnection logic handles this as a normal disconnect/reconnect cycle

### Phase 2 (Growth)

- Static hosting deployment (Vercel/Netlify) with production build
- Extended chart timeframes (7d, 30d)
- Additional fiat currency options (EUR, GBP)
- PWA support for mobile home screen install

### Phase 3 (Expansion)

- Multi-asset support (ETH, SOL, etc.)
- Personal portfolio tracking
- Reusable dark-theme Astro component library
- Public open-source release

### Risk Mitigation

- *Binance WebSocket geo-blocked:* CoinCap fallback provides equivalent functionality. Acceptable latency trade-off for v1.
- *CoinGecko rate limiting:* 60s polling interval stays well within 30 req/min free tier. If hit, degrade gracefully by showing last known data with timestamp.
- *lightweight-charts Astro compatibility:* Library is framework-agnostic vanilla JS â€” low integration risk with Astro client islands.
- *Resource:* Solo developer project. Tight scope minimizes risk. No external dependencies requiring agreements.

## Functional Requirements

### Real-Time Price Display

- FR1: User can view the current BTC/USD price, updated in real-time
- FR2: User can see price updates reflected in the browser tab title
- FR3: User can see a visual indication of price direction (up/down) on each update

### Market Data

- FR4: User can view the 24-hour price change as both a percentage and absolute value
- FR5: User can distinguish positive and negative price changes via color coding
- FR6: User can view the current BTC market capitalization
- FR7: System refreshes supplementary market data (24h change, market cap) periodically without user action

### Price Chart

- FR8: User can view a sparkline chart showing BTC price over the last 24 hours
- FR9: User can hover over the chart to see the price and time at a specific data point
- FR10: Chart displays hourly data points for the 24-hour period

### Connection Resilience

- FR11: System connects to the primary WebSocket data source (Binance) on page load
- FR12: System falls back to a secondary WebSocket source (CoinCap) if the primary fails
- FR13: System automatically reconnects when a WebSocket connection drops
- FR14: User can see a visual indicator when the data connection is lost or reconnecting
- FR15: User can see a stale data indicator when displayed price may be outdated
- FR16: System falls back to REST API polling when WebSocket is unavailable

### Visual Presentation

- FR17: User sees a dark-themed interface with crypto-aesthetic styling on page load
- FR18: User experiences no flash of unstyled or light-themed content on initial load
- FR19: User sees smooth entry animations when data first loads
- FR20: User sees no layout shift as dynamic content populates

### Responsive Experience

- FR21: User can view all dashboard data on desktop screens with a horizontal layout
- FR22: User can view all dashboard data on mobile screens with a vertically stacked layout
- FR23: Dashboard is usable and readable across all supported modern browsers

## Non-Functional Requirements

### Performance

- NFR1: Page load (DOMContentLoaded) completes in < 2 seconds on a 4G connection
- NFR2: Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) occurs in < 1.5 seconds
- NFR3: Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) = 0 â€” no visible layout shift during data hydration
- NFR4: Price updates render to DOM within 1 second of WebSocket message receipt
- NFR5: All animations run at 60 FPS with no dropped frames
- NFR6: Lighthouse performance score â‰¥ 90
- NFR7: Total hydrated JavaScript bundle < 100KB gzipped
- NFR8: Chart library (lightweight-charts) < 50KB gzipped

### Integration

- NFR9: Binance WebSocket connection establishes within 500ms of island hydration under normal network conditions
- NFR10: WebSocket reconnection uses exponential backoff starting at 1 second, capping at 30 seconds
- NFR11: CoinGecko REST API polling interval â‰¥ 60 seconds to stay within free tier rate limits (30 req/min)
- NFR12: System tolerates CoinGecko API unavailability by displaying last known data with a timestamp
- NFR13: Binance 24-hour connection expiry handled transparently as a standard reconnect cycle
- NFR14: Fallback from Binance to CoinCap completes without user intervention

### Reliability

- NFR15: Dashboard remains functional (displays last known data) when all external APIs are unreachable
- NFR16: No JavaScript errors or white screens on WebSocket disconnection
- NFR17: Browser tab remains stable during extended sessions (no memory leaks over 24+ hours)

# Story 1.1: Project Initialization & Tooling Setup Status: ready-for-dev ## Story As a developer, I want a fully scaffolded Astro project with TypeScript, Tailwind, and code quality tooling, so that I can start building components with a consistent, well-configured dev environment from the first commit. ## Acceptance Criteria 1. **Astro 6.x with TypeScript strict mode** â€” Project scaffolded using `npm create astro@latest -- --template minimal --typescript strict`. Astro 6.x installed (requires Node 22+). 2. **Tailwind CSS v4 via Vite plugin** â€” Tailwind configured via `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin in `astro.config.mjs`. CSS-first `@theme` configuration in `src/styles/global.css` (no `tailwind.config.js`). 3. **Dependencies installed** â€” `lightweight-charts`, `@fontsource/jetbrains-mono`, `@fontsource/inter`, and `nanostores` installed as production dependencies. 4. **Code quality tooling** â€” ESLint with `eslint-plugin-astro`, Prettier with `prettier-plugin-astro`, and Vitest configured as dev dependencies. 5. **Project structure matches architecture spec** â€” Directories created: `src/pages/`, `src/layouts/`, `src/components/`, `src/lib/stores/`, `src/lib/services/`, `src/lib/utils/`, `src/styles/`. 6. **TypeScript interfaces defined** â€” `src/lib/types.ts` exports `PriceTick`, `MarketData`, `ChartPoint`, and `ConnectionStatus` interfaces. 7. **Dev server works** â€” `npm run dev` starts the Astro dev server successfully on `localhost:4321`. 8. **Build works** â€” `npm run build` produces a static output in `dist/`. ## Tasks / Subtasks - [ ] Task 1: Scaffold Astro project (AC: #1) - [ ] Run `npm create astro@latest -- --template minimal --typescript strict` - [ ] Verify Node 22+ is available - [ ] Verify Astro 6.x installed in package.json - [ ] Task 2: Configure Tailwind CSS v4 (AC: #2) - [ ] Run `npx astro add tailwind` - [ ] Verify `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin in `astro.config.mjs` - [ ] Create `src/styles/global.css` with `@import "tailwindcss"` - [ ] Verify no `tailwind.config.js` exists (CSS-first config only) - [ ] Task 3: Install production dependencies (AC: #3) - [ ] Run `npm install lightweight-charts @fontsource/jetbrains-mono @fontsource/inter nanostores` - [ ] Task 4: Configure code quality tooling (AC: #4) - [ ] Run `npm install -D eslint prettier eslint-plugin-astro prettier-plugin-astro vitest` - [ ] Create `eslint.config.js` with ESLint flat config and `eslint-plugin-astro` - [ ] Create `.prettierrc` with `prettier-plugin-astro` - [ ] Create `vitest.config.ts` - [ ] Add lint/format/test scripts to `package.json` - [ ] Task 5: Create project directory structure (AC: #5) - [ ] Create `src/pages/` (exists from scaffold) - [ ] Create `src/layouts/` - [ ] Create `src/components/` - [ ] Create `src/lib/stores/` - [ ] Create `src/lib/services/` - [ ] Create `src/lib/utils/` - [ ] Create `src/styles/` (if not already created) - [ ] Task 6: Define TypeScript interfaces (AC: #6) - [ ] Create `src/lib/types.ts` with all four interfaces - [ ] Task 7: Create minimal entry files (AC: #7, #8) - [ ] Create `src/pages/index.astro` (imports Layout) - [ ] Create `src/layouts/Layout.astro` (basic HTML shell) - [ ] Verify `npm run dev` starts successfully - [ ] Verify `npm run build` produces `dist/` ## Dev Notes ### Initialization Commands (Exact Sequence) ```bash npm create astro@latest -- --template minimal --typescript strict npx astro add tailwind npm install lightweight-charts @fontsource/jetbrains-mono @fontsource/inter nanostores npm install -D eslint prettier eslint-plugin-astro prettier-plugin-astro vitest ``` **Package manager:** npm (explicit choice â€” no pnpm/yarn). ### TypeScript Interfaces (Exact Definitions) ```typescript // src/lib/types.ts export interface PriceTick { price: number; timestamp: number; direction: 'up' | 'down' | 'neutral'; } export interface MarketData { marketCap: number; change24h: number; changePercent24h: number; high24h: number; low24h: number; } export interface ChartPoint { time: number; value: number; } export type ConnectionStatus = 'connecting' | 'live' | 'reconnecting' | 'stale' | 'fallback'; ``` ### Astro Config Requirements `astro.config.mjs` must have: - `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin - `output: 'static'` (default, but be explicit) ```javascript import { defineConfig } from 'astro/config'; import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite'; export default defineConfig({ vite: { plugins: [tailwindcss()], }, }); ``` ### Vitest Config ```typescript // vitest.config.ts import { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config'; export default defineConfig({ test: { include: ['src/**/*.test.ts'], }, }); ``` ### ESLint Config (Flat Config Format) Use ESLint flat config in `eslint.config.js` with `eslint-plugin-astro`. Do NOT use legacy `.eslintrc` format. ### Project Structure (Complete) ``` src/ â”œâ”€â”€ pages/ â”‚ â””â”€â”€ index.astro â”œâ”€â”€ layouts/ â”‚ â””â”€â”€ Layout.astro â”œâ”€â”€ components/ # Empty â€” populated in later stories â”œâ”€â”€ lib/ â”‚ â”œâ”€â”€ types.ts # Shared TypeScript interfaces â”‚ â”œâ”€â”€ stores/ # Empty â€” populated in Story 2.1 â”‚ â”œâ”€â”€ services/ # Empty â€” populated in Story 2.2 â”‚ â””â”€â”€ utils/ # Empty â€” populated in Story 2.1 â””â”€â”€ styles/ â””â”€â”€ global.css # Tailwind @import only for now ``` ### Anti-Patterns (DO NOT) - Do NOT create a `tailwind.config.js` â€” Tailwind v4 uses CSS-first `@theme` directives - Do NOT create barrel files (`utils/index.ts`) â€” import directly from specific modules - Do NOT add any UI framework (React, Vue, Svelte) â€” all islands use vanilla TypeScript - Do NOT add Playwright yet â€” it's for e2e testing in later stories - Do NOT pre-create store files, service files, or component files beyond `index.astro` and `Layout.astro` â€” those belong to later stories - Do NOT add `theme-constants.ts` yet â€” that's Story 1.2 - Do NOT add content to `global.css` beyond Tailwind import â€” design tokens are Story 1.2 ### Minimal Layout.astro The layout should be a minimal HTML shell for now. Story 1.2 adds design tokens, Story 1.3 adds the responsive grid layout. ```astro --- // src/layouts/Layout.astro --- Metrics ``` ### Minimal index.astro ```astro --- // src/pages/index.astro import Layout from '../layouts/Layout.astro'; ---

Metrics

 ``` ### package.json Scripts Ensure these scripts exist: - `"dev": "astro dev"` - `"build": "astro build"` - `"preview": "astro preview"` - `"test": "vitest run"` - `"test:watch": "vitest"` - `"lint": "eslint src/"` - `"format": "prettier --write src/"` ### Project Structure Notes - This is a greenfield project â€” no existing code to conflict with - All paths follow the architecture spec exactly: [Source: architecture.md#Structure Patterns] - TypeScript interfaces defined exactly per architecture data interfaces: [Source: architecture.md#Data Architecture] - Tailwind v4 CSS-first config per architecture decision: [Source: architecture.md#Selected Starter] ### References - [Source: architecture.md#Selected Starter: Astro Minimal + CLI Integrations] â€” initialization commands, package manager choice, starter rationale - [Source: architecture.md#Structure Patterns] â€” complete project directory structure and file naming rules - [Source: architecture.md#Data Architecture] â€” PriceTick, MarketData, ChartPoint, ConnectionStatus interface definitions - [Source: architecture.md#Implementation Patterns & Consistency Rules] â€” naming conventions, anti-patterns - [Source: prd.md#Technical Architecture] â€” Astro Islands Architecture, static output, TypeScript strict mode - [Source: epics.md#Story 1.1] â€” acceptance criteria source ## Dev Agent Record ### Agent Model Used ### Debug Log References ### Completion Notes List ### File List
